title: Bad error message on empty invalid device mode in API requests
body:
### Issue Description

While trying to use podman with the cluster-api Docker infrastructure provider the API request apparently contained a /dev/fuse:: device (I reported that as a bug here: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/issues/12485 ). This resulted in the unhelpful error message (both in the API response and the logs)
```
Request Failed(Internal Server Error): container create: invalid device mode: 
```
The same problem can be reproduced on the CLI with e.g.

```
# podman run -it --rm --device /dev/fuse:: debian:bookworm /bin/bash
Error: invalid device mode:
```
It would be useful in situations like this if the context (the entire device mapping string) was included in the error message. In the logs I would even suggest including more info from the request since most of the other information (e.g. the container name) only show up at significantly higher log levels.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Request creation of container with device mapping /dev/fuse:: either from CLI or via API
2. observe error "invalid device mode: "



### Describe the results you received

invalid device mode:

### Describe the results you expected

Something along the lines of:

In device mapping "/dev/fuse::" for container "foo": device mappings do not allow empty device mode.
Either omit the second : or specify a valid device mode.
